arts.ttl: Manchester United v Leicester, Nottingham Forest v Newcastle, and more: football – live
arts.sum: Premier League 2pm GMT kick-offs and much more Live scores | Share your thoughts with Yara via email Paul MacInnes with some big news that will certainly affect the Premier League. Proposed changes to the football regulator that would ensure clubs could not be sold to nation states are to be put before the House of Lords, as legislation returns to parliament this week. Nineteen changes to the football governance bill have been proposed by Fair Game, an organisation of 34 men’s clubs that argues for a more sustainable approach to running the national sport. Other proposals include the addition of a human rights component to owners’ and directors’ tests and a mandate to disclose the source of an owner’s funds. The text of the proposed amendment on state ownership says: “The Bill must exclude the possibility that an owner of a club could be a state or state-controlled person or entity. Forest have had the third-lowest possession in the league this season – but it works. Or at least, works for them. A club with a more heightened sense of self-worth might insist on having more of the ball, on something more sophisticated, but simplicity has its own charm. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Report: Nottingham Forest miss out on 13-goal striker now valued at £60m and wanted by Liverpool
Newcastle United and Liverpool handed potential boost in transfer pursuit of La Liga star
arts.sum: The report, via Sport Witness, suggests the Premier League clubs have received a potential boost in their attempts to sign Bade as Sevilla may be forced to cash in on the centre-back to raise funds to aid their financial situation. Bade, whose previous clubs include Le Havre, Lens, Rennes and Nottingham Forest (loan), has a reported €60m (£50m) release clause in his current contract.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United squad revealed for Nottingham Forest clash with one key addition
arts.sum: Murphy's return gives Howe the option of switching Anthony Gordon back to his favoured left flank, but that would mean disrupting the Joelinton-Joe Willock combination which worked so well against Arsenal. Eddie Howe will take a strong squad to the City Ground for Newcastle United's final game before the November international break.
